_draft.note: lens/contradiction have no legacy source; take is a mechanical concat scaffold. Author from concept-lens-vocabulary.md before promoting to gold.

read.image: Compton draws the line. The block party is on the right side of it.
read.take: A Compton verdict on a rivalry, delivered loud enough that the rest of the country is welcome to overhear. The insults are not the point, the certainty is, and Kendrick stitches them into a beat the block can dance to. By the time he is calling Drake a colonizer the diss has already become an anthem, which is its own kind of cruelty. It struts. Every line lands like it already knows it won, and it wants you dancing while it twists the knife.

arc.label: Intro
arc.scene: A whispered 'I see dead people' breaks the silence, and Mustard's beat rolls in easy, almost friendly, the swing of a smile before the first punch.

arc.label: Verse 1
arc.scene: The names start landing. Drake by name, the 'A minor' pun, the receipts in order. He is not arguing, he is reading a verdict.

arc.label: Hook
arc.scene: 'They not like us' becomes a whole city singing back. You are either in the room or you are the target.

arc.label: Verse 2
arc.scene: He does not argue, he convicts. 'You not a colleague, you a fuckin' colonizer' lands like a sentence handed down.

arc.label: Bridge
arc.scene: The taunts turn into a dance. The crowd is chanting it back at him before the next line even drops.

arc.label: Outro
arc.scene: The beat coasts out. Nothing is left to argue. Compton has already moved on to the next song of the summer.

lines.insight: Four words doing the work of a manifesto. The line draws a circle and leaves someone outside it.

lines.line: Tryna strike a chord and it's probably A minor
lines.insight: The cruelest bar wears a pun, so the room laughs half a second before the meaning lands.

lines.line: No, you not a colleague, you a fuckin' colonizer
lines.insight: The verdict, in plain language. Not collaboration, extraction.

lines.line: Say, Drake, I hear you like 'em young
lines.insight: The line that turned the diss into something the song could no longer be taken back from. Direct address, on purpose.

read.texture: A West Coast bounce that never breaks a sweat. The bass is round and warm, the beat keeps grinning, and the menace lives entirely in how relaxed it all sounds.

themes.name: community defense
themes.description: Compton is not a backdrop here, it is the proof of who belongs and who is only visiting.

themes.name: moral indictment
themes.description: The charges are pointed and specific. The 'A minor' pun, the 'colonizer' line. He is not asking for a debate, he is filing the receipts.

themes.name: celebration as weapon
themes.description: The ugliest charges arrive on the bounciest beat, so the crowd chants them back before they finish flinching.

themes.name: West Coast claim
themes.description: The bounce, the cadence, the swing. Every choice plants a flag on a coast Drake does not get to share.
